content:
4 min read
Just now

--

If you are thinking about studying computer science, enrolling in a coding bootcamp, or switching careers into tech right now, you are probably feeling a little bit of whiplash.
A few years ago, “learn to code” was the ultimate golden ticket. Today, the headlines paint a completely different picture: “AI writes nearly half of all code on GitHub,” “Entry-level tech hiring remains highly competitive,” and “Tech companies prioritize efficiency.”
It’s natural to ask yourself: Am I studying for a career that won’t exist by the time I graduate?
The short answer is no, tech is not dead. In fact, a computer science degree remains one of the highest-ROI paths available. However, the job itself has completely mutated. If you study tech in 2026 the exact same way people did in 2021, you will struggle.
Here is the honest reality of what it means to study tech today, what has changed, and how to position yourself to win.
1. The Era of the “Code Monkey” is Over; The Era of the “Product Engineer” is Here
To understand if tech is worth studying, you have to look at what AI is actually doing.

• What AI is great at: Syntax. It knows where the semicolon goes, how to write a…
